options.label: Reusable instructions that can be activated to load specific context
Markdown guides loaded automatically in every interaction to direct the agent
Installable packages that combine documentation and MCP servers
Automations that trigger agent actions in response to IDE events

questions.explanation: Skills in Kiro are reusable instructions that can be activated to load specific context and instructions, enabling specialized behaviors on demand.

questions.text: Skills can be defined both at the user level (~/.kiro/skills/) and at the workspace level (.kiro/skills/).
questions.explanation: Skills can be added to ~/.kiro/skills/ for global user use or to .kiro/skills/ for workspace-specific use, offering flexibility in organization.

questions.text: You want Kiro to always follow a specific logging pattern when creating services. Which feature is most appropriate?

options.label: Create a Skill with logging instructions that can be activated when needed
Add comments in each service file explaining the pattern
Create a custom linter that checks the pattern
Write a script that automatically adds logging after creation

questions.explanation: A Skill is ideal for this case as it allows defining reusable instructions about the logging pattern that Kiro will follow whenever the skill is activated during service creation.
